reasons: Belerick's taunt can hold enemies in place long enough for Odette to start Swan Song damage.
Odette benefits from Belerick because he can front line and reduce the chance that enemies immediately dive her.

proof.summary: Belerick taunts and holds enemies near him, while Odette follows with AoE magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Belerick catches multiple enemies before Odette commits.
Odette casts from a safe angle behind Belerick.
failureCases: Belerick catches only one low-value target.
Odette is interrupted or forced to disengage.
Enemy spreads before Belerick's taunt connects.

reasons: Belerick can occupy the front line and force enemies to fight inside Pharsa's long-range damage zone.
Pharsa can follow Belerick's taunt from safer distance.

proof.summary: Belerick creates front-line control, while Pharsa rains damage from range onto enemies forced to respond to him.
worksBestWhen: Belerick fights near a choke or objective.
Pharsa has a clear angle before Belerick taunts.
failureCases: Pharsa is flanked during her follow-up.

reasons: Belerick can hold the front line and keep enemies occupied in front of Ixia.
Ixia benefits from grouped or controlled enemies because her teamfight damage pattern wants enemies inside her firing area.

proof.summary: Belerick absorbs and controls the enemy front, while Ixia follows from behind with sustained teamfight damage.
worksBestWhen: Belerick keeps enemies inside Ixia's attack area.
Ixia positions safely before Belerick commits.
failureCases: Enemy divers bypass Belerick and reach Ixia.
Belerick pulls enemies away from Ixia's firing angle.
Ixia is forced to reposition during her key damage window.

reasons: Belerick can keep enemy attention away from Hanabi while she deals damage from the backline.
Hanabi's bouncing attacks become more valuable when Belerick forces enemies to fight close together.

proof.summary: Belerick controls and absorbs the front line, while Hanabi punishes grouped enemies with bouncing basic attacks and skills.
worksBestWhen: Belerick stands between Hanabi and enemy divers.
Hanabi has enough items to contribute sustained damage.
failureCases: Hanabi is behind in item timing.
Enemies split and avoid bounce value.

reasons: Belerick can start or absorb a teamfight before Claude enters with Blazing Duet.
Claude benefits when Belerick forces enemies to spend attention and control on the frontline first.

proof.summary: Belerick creates a durable front-line fight, while Claude enters afterward to apply AoE marksman damage.
worksBestWhen: Belerick forces key enemy control before Claude dives.
Claude keeps Battle Mirror Image available for entry or exit.
failureCases: Claude enters before Belerick creates space.
Enemy saves hard control for Claude.
Belerick's taunt pulls enemies away from Claude's damage path.
